verses.text: Paul goes through Macedonia and Greece, abiding three months. Tireless travel. He labours to confirm the churches.
Paul goes through Macedonia and Greece, abiding three months. Tireless travel. He labours to confirm the churches.
Paul goes through Macedonia and Greece, abiding three months. Tireless travel. He labours to confirm the churches.
Companions go ahead to Troas; they sail after the unleavened bread. The company. He does not work alone.
Companions go ahead to Troas; they sail after the unleavened bread. The company. He does not work alone.
Companions go ahead to Troas; they sail after the unleavened bread. The company. He does not work alone.
On the first day they break bread; Paul preaches till midnight; Eutychus falls from the loft, dead. A long sermon. The young man's sleep ends in a fall.
On the first day they break bread; Paul preaches till midnight; Eutychus falls from the loft, dead. A long sermon. The young man's sleep ends in a fall.
On the first day they break bread; Paul preaches till midnight; Eutychus falls from the loft, dead. A long sermon. The young man's sleep ends in a fall.
Paul embraces him, 'his life is in him'; they bring him alive, and are comforted. Life restored. God turns sorrow into comfort.
Paul embraces him, 'his life is in him'; they bring him alive, and are comforted. Life restored. God turns sorrow into comfort.
Paul embraces him, 'his life is in him'; they bring him alive, and are comforted. Life restored. God turns sorrow into comfort.
They sail by Miletus, for Paul hastes to Jerusalem by Pentecost. Set purpose. He presses toward his appointed task.
They sail by Miletus, for Paul hastes to Jerusalem by Pentecost. Set purpose. He presses toward his appointed task.
They sail by Miletus, for Paul hastes to Jerusalem by Pentecost. Set purpose. He presses toward his appointed task.
They sail by Miletus, for Paul hastes to Jerusalem by Pentecost. Set purpose. He presses toward his appointed task.
He calls the Ephesian elders, Ye know how I served the Lord 'with all humility... and many tears.' Faithful pastor. He labours with humility and grief for souls.
He calls the Ephesian elders, Ye know how I served the Lord 'with all humility... and many tears.' Faithful pastor. He labours with humility and grief for souls.
He calls the Ephesian elders, Ye know how I served the Lord 'with all humility... and many tears.' Faithful pastor. He labours with humility and grief for souls.
I kept back nothing profitable, testifying 'repentance toward God, and faith toward our Lord Jesus.' Full message. He preached the whole gospel without flinching.
I kept back nothing profitable, testifying 'repentance toward God, and faith toward our Lord Jesus.' Full message. He preached the whole gospel without flinching.
Bound in the spirit I go to Jerusalem; 'none of these things move me... that I might finish my course with joy.' Steadfast. He counts not his life dear for Christ.
Bound in the spirit I go to Jerusalem; 'none of these things move me... that I might finish my course with joy.' Steadfast. He counts not his life dear for Christ.
Bound in the spirit I go to Jerusalem; 'none of these things move me... that I might finish my course with joy.' Steadfast. He counts not his life dear for Christ.
Ye shall see my face no more; 'I am pure from the blood of all men,' having declared all God's counsel. Clear conscience. He hid nothing of God's will.
Ye shall see my face no more; 'I am pure from the blood of all men,' having declared all God's counsel. Clear conscience. He hid nothing of God's will.
Ye shall see my face no more; 'I am pure from the blood of all men,' having declared all God's counsel. Clear conscience. He hid nothing of God's will.
'Take heed... to all the flock, over the which the Holy Ghost hath made you overseers'; wolves shall come. Guard the flock. The shepherds must watch against false teachers.
'Take heed... to all the flock, over the which the Holy Ghost hath made you overseers'; wolves shall come. Guard the flock. The shepherds must watch against false teachers.
'Take heed... to all the flock, over the which the Holy Ghost hath made you overseers'; wolves shall come. Guard the flock. The shepherds must watch against false teachers.
Watch, remembering I warned you 'night and day with tears'; 'I commend you to God, and to the word of his grace.' Tearful charge. He leaves them in God's keeping.
Watch, remembering I warned you 'night and day with tears'; 'I commend you to God, and to the word of his grace.' Tearful charge. He leaves them in God's keeping.
I coveted no man's silver; these hands ministered; 'It is more blessed to give than to receive.' Selfless example. He worked to give, not to gain.
I coveted no man's silver; these hands ministered; 'It is more blessed to give than to receive.' Selfless example. He worked to give, not to gain.
I coveted no man's silver; these hands ministered; 'It is more blessed to give than to receive.' Selfless example. He worked to give, not to gain.
He kneels and prays; they weep and kiss him, sorrowing they shall see his face no more. Tender parting. Love makes the farewell hard.
He kneels and prays; they weep and kiss him, sorrowing they shall see his face no more. Tender parting. Love makes the farewell hard.
